name: Rootstock Cloud ERP API
what_it_does: Rootstock Cloud ERP REST API for manufacturing and distribution ERP built natively on Salesforce. Enables AI agents to manage production order creation, scheduling, and work-in-process tracking for manufacturing execution, handle bill of materials (BOM) and product structure data for manufacturing engineering, access inventory management and warehouse operations data for distribution and manufacturing supply chain, retrieve customer order management and delivery scheduling for order fulfillment, handle purchasing and supplier management for procurement automation, access job costing and production cost analytics for manufacturing financial management, retrieve MRP (Material Requirements Planning) and demand forecasting data for planning automation, manage subcontract and outside processing workflow data for supply chain coordination, handle shop floor work order routing and labor tracking data for production operations, and integrate manufacturing ERP data with CRM, IoT, quality management, and supply chain platforms.
use_cases: Automating production orders from manufacturing planning agents
Tracking inventory from distribution management agents
Managing customer orders from Salesforce CRM-integrated agents
Analyzing job costs from manufacturing financial agents
not_for: Non-Salesforce environments without Salesforce platform dependency
Process manufacturing without discrete manufacturing workflow focus
Enterprise large-scale manufacturing requiring standalone ERP
best_when: A mid-market discrete manufacturer or distributor using Salesforce CRM and Rootstock ERP wants AI agents to automate production order management, inventory tracking, customer order fulfillment, job costing, and supply chain management fully integrated with Salesforce.
avoid_when: COMPLIANCE RISK: Rootstock is built on Salesforce, inheriting Salesforce API limits — automated ERP operations can exhaust Salesforce org-level API limits affecting CRM and customer-facing operations. Automated BOM and routing changes require engineering change order (ECO) process for regulated manufacturers (AS9100, ISO 13485). Salesforce-native data model means manufacturing data is in Salesforce objects — data governance must apply Salesforce security model to sensitive manufacturing data.

auth.notes: Rootstock uses OAuth 2.0 (Salesforce OAuth — inherits Salesforce authentication). Developer portal at developer.rootstock.com. San Ramon, California HQ. VC-backed (private). 100% Salesforce-native ERP — requires Salesforce org. 500+ manufacturers and distributors. Strong in mid-market discrete manufacturing and high-tech distribution. Competes with Plex, Epicor, and SYSPRO for mid-market manufacturing ERP.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — annual subscription plus Salesforce platform costs
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual subscription per user; Salesforce license required separately
pricing.notes: San Ramon, California. Founded 2008. Private (VC-backed). 100% Salesforce-native ERP. 500+ customers. Strong in high-tech, electronics, and mid-market manufacturing. Salesforce AppExchange certified. Competes with Plex (cloud manufacturing), Epicor (mid-market ERP), and Infor SyteLine for manufacturing ERP.

performance.rate_limits: Inherits Salesforce API limits; Rootstock-specific limits additional

known_agent_gotchas: COMPLIANCE RISK: Salesforce API limits are shared; automated ERP operations must be throttled to preserve API capacity for CRM and other Salesforce-dependent business operations
BOM engineering change control — BOM and routing changes require ECO process for regulated manufacturers; automated BOM modifications must go through engineering change workflow
Salesforce security model — Rootstock ERP data is in Salesforce objects; field-level security (FLS), object permissions, and sharing rules from Salesforce apply to ERP data access
MRP batch processing — MRP runs are batch processes; automated MRP triggering should occur during off-peak hours and results must be reviewed before releasing purchase requisitions
Salesforce version dependency — Rootstock follows Salesforce seasonal release cadence; Salesforce platform changes can affect Rootstock API behavior; test after each Salesforce release
Mid-market API maturity — Rootstock serves mid-market manufacturing; API documentation and developer tooling are less mature than enterprise ERP (SAP, Oracle)
agent_readiness.security_notes: Cloud manufacturing ERP. SOC2. OAuth2/Salesforce. US/EU. Manufacturing production and financial data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Manufacturing ERP. SOC2. OAuth2/Salesforce. US/EU. Manufacturing and production data.
